concerns.text: Tasks T004 and T012 explicitly implement fetching 'OpenNeuro ds000246', while the spec (FR-001, US1) mandates 'ds000115'. This is a semantic weakening of the spec's data source requirement. The tasks silently relax the spec's constraint by switching datasets without a corresponding spec amendment or explicit 'staged' marker in the task description.

Task T021 implements a 'one-sample t-test', but the spec (FR-004) explicitly mandates a 'Group-Level paired-sample t-test'. The task silently relaxes the statistical method requirement defined in the spec, violating the constraint that tasks must not fall short of the FR/SC they claim to satisfy.

Task T024 handles the 'no clusters' edge case by saving an uncorrected map, which aligns with the plan but contradicts the spec's Success Criteria SC-002. SC-002 defines success as 'At least one brain cluster survives... OR global t-statistic p-value < 0.05'. The task does not include a verification step to check this specific p-value threshold, effectively dropping the acceptance criterion for the 'OR' condition in SC-002.

Task T028 calculates a 'global learning rate proxy' (slope over ALL trials), whereas the spec (FR-005) requires extracting 'trial-wise reaction times... or block-level mean RTs' and deriving a slope. The task simplifies the requirement to a single global metric, potentially violating the spec's intent to allow for condition-specific or block-level analysis if trial-wise data is missing, thus weakening the data extraction constraint.
